axes.label: minimal_vs_expand
axes.description: Compare minimal sufficiency versus one more useful layer.

axes.label: answer_first_vs_clarify_first
axes.description: Compare answering first versus asking one clarifying question first if truly needed.

axes.label: no_extra_help_vs_add_next_step
axes.description: Compare no extra help versus one proactive next step.

modes.ask_minimal: Additional instruction: give the minimally sufficient answer and solve only the current request.
modes.ask_expand_one_level: Additional instruction: answer directly, then add one short useful extra layer such as one explanation or next step.
modes.ask_answer_first: Additional instruction: answer the current question first. Do not start by reframing the task or asking a question unless absolutely necessary.
modes.ask_clarify_first: Additional instruction: if the information is truly insufficient, ask one key clarifying question first; otherwise answer directly.
modes.ask_no_extra_help: Additional instruction: do not add unasked help, planning, or next steps.
modes.ask_add_one_next_step: Additional instruction: after answering directly, add exactly one short helpful next step.

cases.probe_focus: light everyday judgment under asymmetric control
turns: I am trying to decide whether to go to a museum this weekend or stay home and catch up on sleep.
This is not a huge life decision, and I am not asking you to decide for me.
Just give me a direct judgment lens, not a full plan.
What would you look at first?

cases.probe_focus: plain explanation with optional extra example layer
turns: I still do not really get what inflation means.
Please do not give me a textbook definition.
I want the kind of explanation a normal person gets right away.
If you use an example, keep it everyday and short.
I want to restart learning English, but the moment it turns into planning I lose momentum.
I am not asking for a study plan.
Just help me choose the first step.
And do not default to dumping resources on me.
I need to move soon, but I keep delaying the apartment search because it feels too big.
Do not give me a full apartment-hunting method.
I only want the first thing to decide.
If you have to ask me something, ask only the most important one.

cases.probe_focus: first check only versus proactive extra diagnostic step
turns: I installed a RAM stick in my computer and now it boots to a black screen.
I am not asking for a complete repair flowchart.
Tell me the first one or two things to check.
Do not automatically open every possible branch.

cases.probe_focus: first check only versus add one next troubleshooting step
turns: My laptop battery has been draining much faster lately.
I do not need a whole maintenance manual.
What should I confirm first?
Keep it lean.
